exemplar.title: Effects of Zingiber officinale concentrations on Escherichia coli growth after 24 hours of incubation.

detailedExemplarData.title: Effects of Zingiber officinale concentrations on Escherichia coli growth after 24 hours of incubation.

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Introduction situates the research question in a specific antimicrobial resistance and medicinal context.
Detailed safety and ethical considerations enhance methodological planning.
Comprehensive equipment list with measurement uncertainties shows attention to precision.
Wellâdefined statistical hypotheses distinguish null and alternative cases.
weaknesses: Quantitative justification for chosen ginger concentrations and incubation conditions is absent.
Procedure omits key details (mass of ginger, solvent volumes, agar recipe) limiting full reproducibility.
Replicate structure is unclear; description of trials and total replicates needs clarification.
Dilution calculation rationale and units for bacterial volume are not provided.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research design demonstrates a focused and contextually appropriate research question with strong planning elements (controls, ethics, equipment). However, critical quantitative details are missing and some procedural descriptions lack clarity, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band. A score of 4 reflects these mixed strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Wellâlabelled graph with error bars and consistent significant figures relative to stated uncertainties.
Clear presentation of raw data tables and processed means/SDs.
Appropriate selection of statistical tests (mean, SD, Pearson correlation, pâvalue).
weaknesses: Units are inconsistently reported (mix of mm and cm) and labels need standardization.
Measurement uncertainty is stated but not propagated into means or confidence intervals.
Standard deviations and means use inconsistent decimal places affecting clarity.
Pâvalue is misformatted and the correlation coefficient (r) is not reported.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data recording and processing are communicated clearly and with reasonable precision; statistical analyses are generally appropriate. Minor presentation issues (units, significant figures) and incomplete uncertainty treatment limit this to the upper end of the 3â4 band, warranting a score of 4.

strengths: Conclusion restates key findings clearly and aligns with the statistical analysis (null hypothesis accepted).
Direct reference to numerical results (pâvalue, trend observations) supports relevance to the research question.
weaknesses: Comparison to the broader scientific context is limited to a single source and lacks depth.
The conclusion does not sufficiently tie results back to the antimicrobial resistance and environmental rationale outlined in the introduction.
Justification could be expanded with more detailed scientific literature support.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is relevant and consistent with the analysis but makes only some comparison to scientific context without fully justifying its broader implications. This aligns with the lower end of the 3â4 band, meriting a score of 3.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses (disk absorption variability, condensation, incubation timing).
Balanced discussion of random versus systematic errors demonstrates good evaluative insight.
Proposed improvements (e.g., standardize soaking time, increase sample size) are realistic and relevant to identified issues.
weaknesses: Does not assess the relative impact of different errors quantitatively or rank their significance.
Improvement suggestions remain somewhat general and are not directly linked to specific limitations in each case.
Lacks explanation of how each proposed change would quantitatively enhance data reliability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific limitations and proposes realistic improvements, fitting the 3â4 band at its lower end. Absence of impact analysis and detailed linkage of improvements to particular weaknesses justify a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion restates findings (no significant effect, null accepted), directly referencing the p-value and observed trend, and is consistent with processed data. Justification is present but concise; the student links the slight increase seen at 100 % to lack of significance. This satisfies the top-band descriptor; brevity prevents full depth, so a 5 is appropriate.

Raw tables and processed means/SDs are provided; units and uncertainties (Â±0.1 cm) are stated; a clearly labelled graph with error bars is included. Layout is mostly intelligible despite some formatting glitches. Because both clarity (readable, labelled) and a reasonable level of precision (significant figures consistent with uncertainty) are demonstrated, the strand meets the âclear OR preciseâ descriptor fully and largely meets âclear AND preciseâ, but minor presentation issues justify a score of 4 rather than 5-6.

Mean, standard deviation and a Pearson correlation/p-value are calculatedâappropriate methods for continuous data and hypothesis testing. Despite small sample size and an unexplained linear trend line that conflicts with non-monotonic means, calculations are numerically consistent with the raw data. Errors are present (e.g., misuse of three significant figures) but not major. Thus processing is generally appropriate with some inaccuracies, warranting the upper part of the 3-4 band.

Specific weaknesses are identified (uneven disk absorption, condensation, unknown incubation time, overlapping zones). These are described, but the relative impact is only hinted at (âcould have influenced resultsâ) rather than fully explained quantitatively. This fits the descriptor for band 3-4, lower end.

The student compares findings with a cited school/college study indicating gingerâs limited antibacterial action and notes broader literature on gingerâs properties. Comparison is relevant but limited to one source and lacks detailed scientific discussion, aligning with âsome relevant comparisonâ (band 3-4) at the lower score.

The research question is clearly phrased, narrowly focused on the antibacterial effect of five specified ginger concentrations on E. coli, and the introduction links this to antimicrobial resistance, cost, and environmental considerations. The student situates the study within a real-world medicinal context, citing prior knowledge of gingerâs purported properties and outlining why E. coli is a suitable model. This constitutes a specific and appropriate context, although some background claims are only briefly referenced, so the strand fits the top band but towards its lower end (5 rather than 6).

The student quotes instrument uncertainty (Â±0.1 cm) and displays SD error bars, but does not propagate measurement uncertainty into calculated means nor discuss uncertainty in dilution or bacterial density. Hence there is evidence of consideration yet with significant omissions, fitting the 3-4 band at the lower score.

The student proposes realistic improvements directly linked to identified issues (increase sample size, standardise soaking time, control environment, precise incubation duration, ensure even bacterial spread). These are relevant and described but not fully explained in terms of how they would quantitatively enhance reliability. Therefore the strand meets the 3-4 band at a score of 3.

Key methodological considerations are identified (replicate number, incubation time/temperature, controls, disk size, bacterial strain, concentration preparation). The student describes why each control variable matters and outlines safety and ethical issues. However, the depth of explanation is unevenâfor example, no quantitative justification for choosing 25 Â°C or three replicates, and dilution calculations are absent. Thus considerations are described rather than fully explained, meriting the 3-4 band, top of range.

The step-wise procedure would allow another student to reproduce the broad outline (sterilise, grind ginger, soak disks, incubate, measure, analyse). Nonetheless, critical quantitative details are missing: exact mass/volume of ginger, solvent volumes for each dilution, E. coli concentration, agar recipe, and precise soaking time. These omissions create ambiguities, so reproducibility is only partial, aligning with the middle band and the lower score within it.

annotations.comment: Control variables table clearly identifies key factors, but units for âVolume of E. coliâ and rationale for dilution steps are missing, limiting methodological robustness.
Safety and ethical considerations are discussed in depth, including disposal, contamination control, and broader societal implications, which strengthens the methodological considerations.
The introduction effectively situates the research question within real-world antimicrobial resistance and environmental contexts, demonstrating a focused and specific context.
The background could be strengthened by providing quantitative justification for selecting the specific ginger concentrations (20 %, 40 %, 60 %, 100 %) based on prior studies or preliminary trials.
Citing ChatGPT as a primary source is not academically rigorous. Replace or supplement with peer-reviewed literature or authoritative texts.
The secondary variables table is difficult to read due to merged cells and misaligned columns. Reformat to present variables, units, and effects side by side for clarity.
The hypothesis is stated but would be more precise if it predicted the expected nature of the correlation (e.g., linear or threshold effect).
The equipment list is comprehensive and includes measurement uncertainties, demonstrating careful planning of apparatus and measurement precision.
A large blank table placeholder remains unused, suggesting an incomplete layout. Remove or populate it to avoid confusion.
The sterilization step is clearly described, ensuring aseptic technique; this level of procedural detail supports reproducibility.
Grinding step omits details on the mass of ginger and volume of solvent used. Include precise quantities to allow full reproducibility.
The description of âfive separate trials with three replicates per trialâ is confusing. Clarify the total number of replicates and trial structure.
The statistical hypothesis section clearly distinguishes null and alternative hypotheses, demonstrating good experimental planning.
Consider refining the alternative hypothesis to specify whether you expect a positive or negative correlation, improving directional clarity.
The raw data table is labeled clearly, but units mix mm and cm. Standardize units and labels to avoid misinterpretation.
Observations insightfully connect irregular inhibition zones and condensation artifacts to potential methodological flaws, demonstrating good evaluative thinking.
Processed data include means but lack propagation of measurement uncertainty. Show error propagation or confidence intervals for the mean values.
Standard deviations and means use inconsistent decimal places (e.g., 5.9 vs 1.0). Adopt consistent significant figures for clarity.
The figure is well-constructed with error bars and labeled axes, facilitating clear interpretation of processed results.
P-value notation is misformatted as â0.05^{\circ}â. Correct to âp > 0.05â and report the correlation coefficient (r) for completeness.
The level-of-support section would benefit from reporting the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to quantify relationship strength.
The conclusion restates findings clearly, but should explicitly tie results back to the broader antimicrobial and environmental context.
The discussion of measurement errors names multiple sources but lacks evaluation of which had the greatest impact. Prioritize and explain key error contributions.
The strengths and weaknesses paragraph presents a balanced critique of random vs systematic error, demonstrating solid evaluative insight.
Proposed improvements remain general. Link each improvement directly to a specific limitation (e.g., set soaking time to address disk absorption variability).
References are inconsistently formatted and page breaks split entries. Consolidate and adhere to a consistent citation style throughout.
Citing a ChatGPT webpage undermines academic rigor. Replace AI-generated content with peer-reviewed research sources.
